title: chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. ciliatus
intro: Chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. ciliatus, also known as the three-flowered broom or white broom, is a plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and coumarins. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic, as well as showing potential in preventing certain types of cancer. Chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. ciliatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
